description: Two CMR commons that together compose the set's named pirate-cohort-at-common pair. Both share the Human Pirate creature subtype, both depict the Brazen-Coalition-vessel-deck setting (Admiral commanding from the wheel vs. crew counting Treasure haul on deck), both are CMR-original or CMR-reprinted pirate commons. The pirate-cohort flavor cluster is structurally adjacent to (but distinct from) the Azure-Fleet-Admiral's Monarch-at-common cluster — this edge is the pirate-as-flavor-wrapper cluster, where the Fall-from-Favor edge is the Monarch-as-mechanic cluster. Both cards already mention each other in their `### Related cards` bibliographies; the formal `## Connections` edge is the structural promotion.

other_candidates_considered.verdict: deferred — only 2 of 4 Bardock-related prints are vision-enriched (BT4-117 + SD3-04); the P-035 ×2 promo prints and SD3-01 require vision pass before threshold-of-3-enriched-anchors is met

other_candidates_considered.candidate: BT4-117 Time Trauma Masked Saiyan ↔ SD3-04 Encroaching Threat Masked Saiyan 1:1 mirror
other_candidates_considered.verdict: subsumed by E2 saiyan race-node — drawing this 1:1 now would be a redundant edge between two cards both anchored on the new race-node; the race-node IS the cohesion anchor

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Universe-6-tournament cohort (Cabba + Champa + Vados + Hit + Frost + Botamo + Magetta)
other_candidates_considered.verdict: post-vision signal — none of the U6 cards beyond Cabba ×2 have landed enriched vision yet; revisit when more U6 prints enrich
deferred — triangle reinforcement edges are best done as their own dedicated wave; not surfaced here as a low-priority pick

voice_note: Two card-endpoints. Both equal nodes. Must be traversable from either side. Hence the mirror. The sigh is professional, not personal. The flavor of Wizards' deliberate pirate-cohort-at-common design intent does land here cleanly — Sidharth Chaturvedi paints the commanding officer, Josh Hass paints the rank-and-file with their haul, and Bandai's same-set commons-cross-print is unmistakable. Worth the work.
